claim_boundary: This slice produces and qualifies a real freestanding PooleKernel PKELF1 product image and host-executed entry helpers; it is not the former empty fixture.
This standalone PKENTRY1 receipt qualifies image construction and host-executed helpers; the separate PKXFER1 receipt owns the QEMU-only live transfer claim.
The temporary framebuffer identity mapping is a mandatory caller precondition discovered in Cycle101; the PBP1 bytes alone do not prove that mapping or its cache policy.
This standalone qualification does not execute privileged port I/O or framebuffer writes; the separate PKXFER1 QEMU receipt executes only emulated devices.

The image halts after validated entry diagnostics and does not initialize descriptor tables, interrupts, memory management, capabilities, scheduling, IPC, services, drivers, PooleGlyph, PDC, or PooleGlass.

Single-host exact reproduction and finite hostile controls do not establish second-host reproducibility, universal correctness, target-firmware support, N6 completion, a bootable ISO, or production readiness.
No signing, key generation, tag, release, merge, firmware change, privileged probe, driver load, or physical-media write is authorized or performed.
open_items: Wire authenticated PooleKernel file discovery, bounded reads, and PKELF1 allocation into PooleBoot.
Install and inspect final page tables, W^X/RELRO permissions, bootstrap stack, read-only PBP1 mapping, and temporary framebuffer mapping.
Replace the QEMU-only unsigned development transfer with an authenticated production PKENTRY1 profile.
Implement GDT, IDT, TSS, exception containment, memory initialization, capability bootstrap, scheduler, IPC, and the initial user-space system.
Reproduce exact product bytes on a second independent builder and qualify target firmware and separately authorized physical media.
Complete the remaining N6 requirements and all downstream N7-N39 production gates.

pkelf1_probe_qualification.host_probe_role: ephemeral host-only differential and exact-byte transport

non_claims: PKPMM4 qualifies only a bounded one-BSP Boot Services reclaim transaction plus the prior guarded metadata and selector-8 allocation lifecycle.
Raw allocate and free APIs retained for predecessor PKVM1 and PKVM2 profiles are non-production and are not covered by the PKPMM4 scrub, metadata, or reclaim claim.
The retained mappings are one guarded five-page supervisor RW/NX metadata arena plus one revoked temporary supervisor RW/NX page alias; no complete direct map or address-space policy is claimed.
Boot Services reclaim is activated only in the bounded qemu64 profile; ACPI reclaim remains held and no positive ACPI activation is claimed.
No concurrent, interrupt-context, or SMP allocator is qualified.
No target hardware, second host, signature, release, or production claim is made.
open_items: Integrate the positive ACPI reclaim transition only after N10 owns ACPI table parsing and explicit table-release evidence.
Implement N9.3/N9.4 virtual layout, page-table ownership, map/unmap, TLB, PCID, guard, and huge-page contracts.
Implement randomized, concurrent, interrupt-context, SMP, fragmentation, quota, and OOM allocator stress.
Implement heap, object caches, kernel stacks, cacheability policy, target hardware, and second-host qualification.
Replace the bounded five-page arena with scalable metadata growth after reclaim, VM ownership, and allocator concurrency contracts exist.
